MOVEMENT.logic: Select objects designed for Interconnectivity or Mechanical Action — things that eject, slide, or change state to allow a flow.
examples: 1980s Hi-Fi Cassette Deck with tape door ejected
Industrial Sliding Gate with exposed pulleys

STRUCTURE.logic: Select objects that represent the Inflexible Backbone of a system — artifacts that define the grid or skeleton.
examples: Steel Suspension Bridge Section with thick cables

SPEED.logic: Select objects that represent Kinetic Velocity or Propulsion — physically built to cut through resistance.
examples: 1980s Jet Turbine Engine with chrome blades

SECURITY.logic: Select objects designed for Hard Containment or Physical Access — heavy, tactile, requiring specific force.
examples: Floor-Standing Steel Safe with mechanical dial
Jewel Box with heavy silver latch

material_types.Beige ABS Plastic: Yellowed 90s computer plastic with subtle scratches and matte texture.
material_types.Phosphorus Glass: Green glowing CRT screen with visible scanlines and glass curvature.
material_types.Bakelite: Deep black polished resin with high-contrast reflections and heavy weight.
material_types.Walnut/Teak: Rich wood grain with a satin finish and worn edges.
material_types.Chrome/Steel: Brushed industrial metal with oil smudges and micro-abrasions.
material_types.Velvet/Fabric: Deeply tufted textile with realistic fiber detail and dust motes.
